body.title: [sprint] Retire the rule-based CSB classifier (wears 'preparse' name) -> source complexity/intent from word-graphs
body.intention: I intend that igor's complexity/intent signals come from word-graphs, not a legacy rule-based classifier that survives under the dead 'preparse' name.
body.description:
**Problem:** The LLM-preparse concept was deleted (T-igor-delete-local-preparse). A rule-based classifier kept under the preparse name SURVIVED that sweep because it still feeds live signals: `_rule_based_csb` / `parse_preparse_csb` / `compute_complexity` (cognition/reasoners/ollama_reasoner.py) produce the `pre`/`complexity` dict consumed by (a) async-job gating via `complexity['score']` (main.py ~4796), (b) memory intent labels via `pre['intent']` (main.py ~4946), and (c) the `pre_csb` prompt injection (inference_gateway.py ~415, base.py preparse_csb field). It predates word-graphs, which now arguably own complexity/intent.

**Proposed shape:** Define word-graph (parsed.complexity / word-graph intent) sourcing for each of the three live consumers, repoint them, THEN delete `_rule_based_csb`/`parse_preparse_csb`/`compute_complexity`, the `preparse_csb` dataclass field (base.py), and the gateway:415 injection. This is the 'larger non-mechanical scope' deliberately split out of the deletion sweep.

**OPEN DESIGN QUESTION (must resolve before build):** what exactly replaces each of the three signals — does the word-graph parse already expose a complexity score + intent, or does that need building? Confirm with Akien / map the word-graph API before repointing. Do NOT delete the CSB layer until replacements are wired and proven.

**Affected files:** cognition/reasoners/ollama_reasoner.py, cognition/reasoners/base.py, cognition/inference_gateway.py, main.py (the three consumer sites).
**Design rules:** Inference Proxy only; canonical memory home; no SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** repoint the three consumers to word-graph sources + delete the CSB layer. Do not touch the already-removed LLM-preparse concept.
**Completion criteria:** the three consumers source from word-graphs (not parse_preparse_csb); `_rule_based_csb`/`parse_preparse_csb`/`compute_complexity` deleted; `grep -ri 'parse_preparse_csb\|_rule_based_csb' unseen_university/devices/igor` -> 0; igor main import + the complexity-gating path proven green. Provable via grep-gate + a test exercising the repointed complexity/intent path.
**Test plan:** grep-gate red->green on the CSB symbols; a unit test asserting complexity['score'] + intent now derive from the word-graph source.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

## Added scope 2026-06-30 (deferred from T-igor-delete-orphaned-reasoner-classes): relocate surviving reasoner consts + finalize files
Once the CSB layer is removed, the reasoner files (ollama_reasoner.py / openrouter_reasoner.py / base.py) hold only module-level consts/helpers. Relocate the surviving consts to a new cognition/inference_config.py (inference_openrouter.py already has a TODO(D327-cfg) marker): OLLAMA_LOCAL_MODEL, OLLAMA_HOST, DEFAULT_MODEL (ollama), MODEL_ALIASES, OPENROUTER_BASE. NOTE: DEFAULT_MODEL name-collision across the two files -> rename to OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL / OR_DEFAULT_MODEL. Repoint importers: runner.py:44-45, inference_ollama.py, inference_openrouter.py, main.py:9380. Then delete the now-empty reasoner files + their shim re-exports. Relocation was deferred because the consts are module-level and the classes could be excised without moving them (simplify).
